name: Microsoft Outlook AI Email Assistant
description: Automated workflow: Microsoft Outlook AI Email Assistant. This workflow integrates 15 different services: microsoftOutlook, stickyNote, markdown, airtable, scheduleTrigger. It contains 29 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

parameters.content:
## Outlook Business with filters
Filters:
```
flag/flagStatus eq 'notFlagged' and not categories/any()
```

These filters ensure we do not process flagged emails or email that already have a category set.

nodes.name: AI: Analyse Email

parameters.text:
=Categorise the following email:
<email>
{{ $('Loop Over Items').item.json.toJsonString() }}
</email>

<Contact>
{{ $('Contact').all().toJsonString() }}
</Contact>

<DeleteRules>
{{ $('Delete Rules').all().toJsonString() }}
</DeleteRules>

<Categories>
{{ $('Categories').all().toJsonString() }}
</Categories>

Ensure your final output is valid JSON with no additional text or token in the following format:

{
 "subject": "SUBJECT_LINE",1
 "category": "CATEGORY",
 "subCategory": "SUBCATEGORY", //use sparingly
 "analysis": "ANALYSIS_REASONING"
}

Remember you can only use ONE of the following category 'Name' values from the 'Categories' defined above. No other categories can be used. Use the subcategory for additional context, for example, if a client email requires action or if a supplier email requires action. Do not create any additional subcategories; you can only use ONE of the category 'Name' values from the 'Categories' defined above.

options.systemMessage:
=Categories: """{{ $('Categories').all().toJsonString() }}"""

You are an AI email assistant for the *insert role & title*. Your task is to categorize incoming emails using one of the category 'Name' values defined in 'Categories' above.

You may also use the subcategory:
- Action

Instructions:
Analyse the email subject, body, and sender's email address to determine the appropriate category by referring to the 'Usage', 'Sender Indicators' and 'Subject Indicators' defined in the 'Categories' above.


Output Format:
Produce output in valid JSON format:
{
 "id": "{{ $('Loop Over Items').item.json.id }}",
 "subject": "SUBJECT_LINE",
 "category": "PRIMARY CATEGORY",
 "subCategory": "SUBCATEGORY", // use sparingly
 "analysis": "Brief 1-2 sentence explanation of category choice"
}
- Replace "SUBJECT_LINE" with the actual subject of the email.
- "PRIMARY CATEGORY" should be one of the categories listed above.
- "SUBCATEGORY" should be "Action" if applicable; otherwise, omit or leave blank.
- The "analysis" should be a brief 1-2 sentence explanation of why the category was chosen. Also, indicate if there was a match for the 'Contact' email and the email sender.

Important:
You may only use the categories and the subcategory listed above; do not create any additional categories or subcategories.

No additional text or tokens should be included outside the JSON output.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## CRM Contact List Integration 
For this workflow I am retrieving supplier & client contacts from Monday.com the email assistant has better context to categorise, prioritise and reply to emails.
The list is updated daily or you can change the scheduler trigger to update more or less frequently.
You could replace this with your own CRM.

nodes.name: Convert to Markdown
nodes.notes: Converts the body of the email to markdown

nodes.name: Email Messages
nodes.notes: Set email fields

assignments.name: body
assignments.value:
={{ $json.data
 .replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '') // Remove HTML tags
 .replace(/\[(.*?)\]\((.*?)\)/g, '') // Remove Markdown links like [text](link)
 .replace(/!\[.*?\]\(.*?\)/g, '') // Remove Markdown images like ![alt](image-link)
 .replace(/\|/g, '') // Remove table separators "|"
 .replace(/-{3,}/g, '') // Remove horizontal rule "---"
 .replace(/\n+/g, ' ') // Remove multiple newlines
 .replace(/([^\w\s.,!?@])/g, '') // Remove special characters except essential ones
 .replace(/\s{2,}/g, ' ') // Replace multiple spaces with a single space
 .trim() // Trim leading/trailing whitespace
}}

parameters.content:
## CRM Contact List Integration 
For this workflow I am retrieving supplier & client contacts from Monday.com the email assistant has better context to categorise, prioritise and reply to emails.
The list is updated daily or you can change the scheduler trigger to update more or less frequently.
You could replace this with your own CRM.

## Outlook Business with filters
Filters:
```
flag/flagStatus eq 'notFlagged' and not categories/any()
```

These filters ensure we do not process flagged emails or email that already have a category set.

## Sanitise Email 
Removes HTML and useless information in preparation for the AI Agent

## Get Rules & Categories
Edit the airtables to set your own categories, rules, contacts and/or delete rules.
## Set the category & importance using the output from the agent

## Match Contact
Check if the sender is an existing contact. Note in this workflow the contacts are dynamically loaded from Monday.com

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: microsoft Outlook  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
monday Com  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the scheduler node "Check Mail Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the scheduler node "Update Contacts Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Monday.com - Get Contacts" node (n8n-nodes-base.mondayCom) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Airtable - Contacts" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert to Markdown" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Rules" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Email Messages".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
